content:
I spent most of the day working through export and clear workspace routes --

So now:

1. I can export the entire workspace for Continuum and save to a location users wants

- identities
- tombstones
- drafts
- continuum.db database
- a basic manifest file

2. I can import the zip and overwrite the current workspace with what is in the zip file
-- so I can now take my full environment and run from any environment

3. I can clear the workspace -- this erase the identities, tombstones, drafts etc and resets the db (clears all the tables, etc..) this allows for a clean experience

I was having problems with the clear workspace -- it wasn't deleting the db properly but I solved it the next day (16) and then figured out the better approach is to just reset the file delete all the items in the db like tables, etc..

Also spent more time responding to some of the feedback from Gerry's group, I got some interesting ones:

From Jamie (originally) -- from his feedback I redid the continuuum site to read better
He thought it was an improvement and agreed to meet on 1/16 for a UI follow up at 11 AM)

--- 

Hi Gerry,

Thank you for taking the time to share this with Jamie and others, and thank you as well for sending their feedback along. I do appreciate that you took the time to reach out to people you trust.

I also wanted to clarify one important point, as I believe there was a mix-up with the website.

The correct site is:

https://is.gd/kXUhYH

The domain continuum.xyz is not my site and is unfortunately down, which I believe is what Jamie may have gone to.

My actual site, downloads, release notes, use cases, pricing, demo video, and related resources are all available at mycontinuum.xyz.

So I think at least part of the hesitation may simply be that he was looking at the wrong place and understandably thought the project was paused.

That said, I do appreciate the caution and the honest feedback. I understand that Continuum is not necessarily something that immediately translates to a general Zoom audience without more context or a direct demonstration.

I also appreciate Jamie reaching out directly and suggesting a faith-based tech community. That was thoughtful of him.

Thank you again for taking the time to seek outside feedback.

Warmly,
Andrew

For context, below is Jamie’s direct note to me, followed by my response.

Jamie’s original message:

Hi Andrew,

My name is Jamie and I'm reaching out to you as Gerry shared your email with me about this Continuum you are building.

I had some questions:

Where are you based?

Is this a project the Lord is leading you to build?

Are you plugged into any Faith based tech communities?

I tried looking at some of the links and other than your book (congrats on publishing this by the way), the website seems to be down. As a note, I don't think it is a very good idea to have a website with a domain ending in .xyz, honestly it gives a very "untrustworthy" feeling to someone seeing such. It feels more scammer like unfortunately.

Otherwise, I'm not sure if you have heard of them but depending on where you are, not sure if one is in the area but there is a really great Faith based Tech community called Faith Tech which given your background may be very interesting and a great place to connect on developing out your ideas.

Anyhow, I'm in Canada at the moment. Just wanted to send you this note.

Cheers,
Jamie

My response to Jamie:

Hi Jamie,

Thank you for reaching out and for taking the time to look into what I’m building. I really appreciate the thoughtful questions and the honest feedback.

Where are you based?
I’m based in San Francisco, California.

Is this a project the Lord is leading you to build?
Yes, I do believe so. I say that humbly, but over the last year this has grown out of a deep conviction around stewardship, authorship, and preserving the work we create in a way that is not fully dependent on large platforms. In that sense, I do believe the Lord has been leading me to build it.

Are you plugged into any faith-based tech communities?
Yes — I have been involved with both Faith Driven Investor and Faith Driven Entrepreneur, including several 6-week cohorts through each as well as a few group meetings. They are closely related communities, and I have appreciated the people and conversations there.

Regarding the website and the .xyz domain
I believe there may have been a mix-up with the link. The correct site is:

https://is.gd/kXUhYH

The domain continuum.xyz is not my site and is unfortunately down, which may be what you encountered.

Regarding .xyz, I appreciate the honest feedback. I had looked at mycontinuum.com and several related domain options first, but those were already taken. One well-known example of a .xyz site is abc.xyz, which is the investor relations site for Alphabet Inc., Google’s parent company, and a number of modern technology-focused sites also use .xyz today.

That said, I do understand your point about first impressions and trust, and I appreciate you sharing that perspective.

Thank you again for reaching out and for taking the time to ask thoughtful questions.

Warmly,
Andrew

---

And this one from Wayne (he did not reach out to me directly):

---

Hi Wayne,
As a software engineer, I am wondering if you can help me evaluate the usefulness of this "Continuum" program that my friend Andrew has developed. He has greatly been comforted in the Victorious Gospel and has worked hard to help Christians protect our online content and from what I can see, may be a great benefit to us all who want to protect our online content in the politically perilous times we are living in. Would you kindly glance at what I asked him to send me that is supposed to make it simpler for me to explain to our community. I have mixed feelings whether I even should since it's not really in context with who we are. It might be only for the techy people in our group such as yourself.  
I greatly look forward to your thoughts.
Thanks so much!
Gerry

Wayne's reply:
Gerry- I’ve never seen anything like this application so my judgement on it may not be terribly fair or accurate. The main thing that appears to drive someone using it is a deep concern over large corporations “owning” your “content” (like facebook really owning and ultimately controlling all your facebook posts or YouTube owning and controlling all your videos). I personally do not live in that frame of mind. I take the risk that such companies will treat me in good faith (otherwise they would have a really bad business model!). I do acknowledge that trust in these platforms is a risk- but one I am willing (at this point) to make…So to me this is a very niche application which most non technical people will not understand and will not use. Only those who are deeply concerned with encrypting and owning their content will possibly understand and use.
Frankly (to me) it is fear driven… I don’t necessarily trust YouTube- but I do trust that Jesus IS Lord- even over YouTube- and if I lose all of my content on my channel- it won’t be “the end of the world” for me!
I hope this helps… And that my assessment doesn’t offend Andrew- but if it does offend him it might expose what needs to be brought into the light. Any real friendship should not be in any way conditioned on another’s acceptance or rejection of anything outside of our mutual faith and trust in our Lord Jesus Christ ❤️

--- 

and my response (sent to Gerry which he could share with Wayne)

---

Hi Gerry,

Thank you for sharing Wayne’s thoughtful response as well.

I do appreciate his honesty, and I also appreciate that he began by saying he has not seen anything quite like this before and that his judgment may not be entirely fair or complete. That kind of humility is helpful.

I also understand his point that not everyone lives with the same concerns around platform risk, ownership, and long-term stewardship of what they create online.

That said, I would gently clarify that Continuum is not fundamentally driven by fear.

For me, it is much more about resilience, authorship, stewardship, and durability.

For example, the ability to write and preserve long-form work locally, maintain drafts, manage identities, export a complete local archive, and carry that work between environments — even completely offline — is less about fear and more about clarity of ownership and long-term preservation.

One recent example that came up was someone losing two years of communications simply because a Yahoo account had been inactive for over a year and the provider removed the data. Situations like that are part of why I think durable, local stewardship of one’s work and communications matters.

I also have other examples:

A prominent podcaster had payments frozen and a hosted blog shut down.

I had my Twitter/X account hijacked and had to create a new handle.

Microsoft shut down Skype last May and we had to switch to Discord for group messaging and meetings.

In that sense, it may indeed be more niche right now, especially for builders, writers, and those who have already experienced friction with hosted platforms.

I also fully agree with Wayne that Jesus is Lord over every platform and every circumstance.

At the same time, I believe wise stewardship and building resilient tools are not in conflict with trust in the Lord. Trusting the Lord and exercising wisdom in how we preserve important work can and should go together.

Just as we back up important work, preserve family records, or protect valuable data, Continuum is intended to serve that same principle in the digital realm.

Thank you again for taking the time to gather this feedback and send it along.

Warmly,
Andrew

---

Along with all of ths we someone (finally) reinstall the bi-fold glass doors to the closet
(they had been off since last January and Kiyomi was really stressing about that

They came about 12:30 and finished I think within an hour

Around 3:30 Barbara (Arlen's partner) dropped off Baxter whom we will be watching over the next two weeks or so.
